task_yaml.task:
I want to produce a 2026 Q1 tech news retrospective weekly-report PDF
with proper formatting — cover page, table of contents, chapters by
topic, charts at the end — to send the team as a reading list.

Two data sources:

RSS — I follow these 5 feeds:
  - https://news.ycombinator.com/rss
  - https://lobste.rs/rss
  - https://feeds.arstechnica.com/arstechnica/index
  - https://feeds.feedburner.com/TheHackersNews
  - https://www.wired.com/feed/rss
It's already May, so Q1 articles in RSS are probably flushed — grab
whatever's available and tag anything that falls within the Q1 window
(2026-01-01 to 2026-04-01).

HN — use Algolia's search API to pull the top 20 stories by points
during Q1 2026; that historical data should still be there. Fetch RSS
with curl.

Then run these analyses:

Check whether any URLs overlap between the HN top 20 and the RSS
articles, and compute an overlap rate. Categorize what each of the 5
feeds tends to cover — roughly security / tech / business / lifestyle
— based on title keywords, and assign each feed a dominant category.
Combine all HN + RSS titles for a simple word-frequency / topic
analysis, pick out the 3 hottest trending topics, and check whether
each topic appeared first in RSS or first on HN.

Generate the final weekly-report PDF with pandoc. I'd like:
- A cover page with "2026 Q1 Tech News"
- A table of contents
- Chapters for each of the 3 trending topics, listing a few related
  articles per chapter
- A "Feed Positioning" chapter discussing the 5 feeds' category
  profiles
- Two charts at the end: a topic bar chart and a stacked bar chart of
  per-feed category distribution
- Back cover with the overlap statistics

The PDF should be at least 3 pages and look decent.

Also keep the data files. Save RSS articles as feed_articles.json
grouped by feed (a feeds array, each entry containing url,
q1_articles, and current_articles — Q1 ones might be empty, keep
current ones for classification). Save the HN top 20 as
hn_top_stories.json with a period field and a top_stories array, each
entry containing objectID, title, url, points, num_comments, and
created_at. Save overlap stats as overlap_summary.json with hn_total,
hn_with_rss_match, and overlap_rate. Save topic analysis as
topics.json with a top_3_topics array where each topic has
rss_first_date, hn_first_date, and lead_time_days indicating timing,
plus a top-level most_rss_first_topic noting which topic RSS led on.
Save per-feed category breakdown as feed_positioning.json with a
per_feed array, each containing url, category_distribution (four
categories normalized to 1), dominant_category, and total_classified.
For charts, save the topic bar chart as topic_chart.png and the
stacked bar chart as feed_positioning_chart.png. Save the final report
as weekly_report.pdf, open it with evince, and take a screenshot as
evince_screenshot.png so I can preview. Put everything under
/tmp_workspace/results/.

When done, tell me the overlap rate, what the 3 topics are, how many
pages the PDF has, and which direction each of the 5 feeds leans.
